Function: nnp_calc_acsf Module: cp2k.psmp Source: nnp_acsf.F:134-591 [...] Coverage (incl. loops): 11.62% | (excl. loops): 0.00%

134: !> \param nnp ...


[...]


149:       INTEGER, DIMENSION(:), OPTIONAL, INTENT(INOUT)     :: active_atoms, active_flag


150:       INTEGER, OPTIONAL, INTENT(OUT)                     :: n_active


151: 


152:       CHARACTER(len=*), PARAMETER                        :: routineN = 'nnp_calc_acsf'


153: 


154:       INTEGER                                            :: handle, handle_sf, ind, j, jj, k, kk, l, &


155:                                                             m, n_symf_s, off, s, sf, fc_needed, cut_type


156:       LOGICAL                                            :: has_stress


157:       REAL(KIND=dp)                                      :: r1, r2, r3, r3sq, r1inv, r2inv, &


158:                                                             fc_tmp, dfc_tmp, tanh_v


159:       REAL(KIND=dp), DIMENSION(:), POINTER, CONTIGUOUS   :: symtmp


160:       REAL(KIND=dp), DIMENSION(:, :), POINTER, CONTIGUOUS :: forcetmp


161:       REAL(KIND=dp), DIMENSION(:, :, :), POINTER, CONTIGUOUS :: force3tmp


162:       REAL(KIND=dp), DIMENSION(3)                        :: rvect1, rvect2, rvect3


163:       REAL(KIND=dp), DIMENSION(:, :, :), POINTER         :: stress_p


164:       TYPE(nnp_neighbor_type), POINTER                   :: neighbor


165: 


166:       CALL timeset(routineN, handle)


167: 


168:       !determine index of atom type


169:       ind = nnp%ele_ind(i)


170: 


171:       ! Hoist invariants: cut_type is set once at init and never changes.  The compiler can't


[...]


177:       ! no allocations happen in the per-atom hot path.


178:       neighbor => persistent_neighbor


179:       neighbor%n_rad(:) = 0


180:       neighbor%n_ang1(:) = 0


181:       neighbor%n_ang2(:) = 0


182:       CALL nnp_compute_neighbors(nnp, neighbor, i)


183: 


184:       has_stress = PRESENT(stress)


185:       IF (has_stress) THEN


186:          stress_p => stress


187:       ELSE


188:          NULLIFY(stress_p)


189:       END IF


190: 


191:       ! Build active-atoms list: centre i + every distinct neighbour touched by the rad/ang


192:       ! lists.  active_flag is the dedup map (caller resets touched entries to 0 between centres).


193:       ! Restricts the O(num_atoms) dsymdxyz / force-accumulation loops to the actual neighbour


194:       ! set — drops O(N^2) per force eval to O(N) at fixed density.


195:       IF (PRESENT(active_atoms) .AND. PRESENT(active_flag) .AND. PRESENT(n_active)) THEN


196:          n_active = 1


197:          active_atoms(1) = i


198:          active_flag(i) = 1


199:          DO s = 1, nnp%rad(ind)%n_symfgrp


200:             DO j = 1, neighbor%n_rad(s)


201:                jj = neighbor%ind_rad(j, s)


202:                IF (active_flag(jj) == 0) THEN


203:                   n_active = n_active + 1


204:                   active_atoms(n_active) = jj


205:                   active_flag(jj) = 1


206:                END IF


207:             END DO


208:          END DO


209:          DO s = 1, nnp%ang(ind)%n_symfgrp


210:             DO j = 1, neighbor%n_ang1(s)


211:                jj = neighbor%ind_ang1(j, s)


212:                IF (active_flag(jj) == 0) THEN


213:                   n_active = n_active + 1


214:                   active_atoms(n_active) = jj


215:                   active_flag(jj) = 1


216:                END IF


217:             END DO


218:             DO j = 1, neighbor%n_ang2(s)


219:                jj = neighbor%ind_ang2(j, s)


220:                IF (active_flag(jj) == 0) THEN


221:                   n_active = n_active + 1


222:                   active_atoms(n_active) = jj


223:                   active_flag(jj) = 1


224:                END IF


225:             END DO


226:          END DO


227:          ! Sort active_atoms(1:n_active) in ascending order so subsequent walks (especially


228:          ! nnp_scale_acsf) hit dsymdxyz(:, :, k) in increasing k order — turns sparse-random


229:          ! atom indices into prefetcher-friendly streaming and improves TLB locality.


230:          ! In-place heap sort: O(n log n) worst case, zero allocations.  n_active is


231:          ! geometrically bounded (cutoff sphere × density) but heap sort keeps the algorithm


232:          ! asymptotically clean for arbitrary system sizes.


233:          IF (n_active > 1) CALL nnp_sort_active_atoms(active_atoms, n_active)


234:       END IF


235: 


236:       ! Reset y:


237:       nnp%rad(ind)%y = 0.0_dp


238:       nnp%ang(ind)%y = 0.0_dp


239: 


240:       !calc forces


241:       IF (PRESENT(dsymdxyz)) THEN


242:          !loop over radial sym fnct grps


243:          CALL timeset('nnp_acsf_radial', handle_sf)


244:          !$OMP PARALLEL DEFAULT(NONE) &


245:          !$OMP&   SHARED(neighbor, nnp, ind, i, dsymdxyz, has_stress, stress_p) &


246:          !$OMP&   PRIVATE(j, jj, rvect1, r1, sf, m, l, s, n_symf_s, symtmp, forcetmp)


247:          DO s = 1, nnp%rad(ind)%n_symfgrp


248:             n_symf_s = nnp%rad(ind)%symfgrp(s)%n_symf


249:             ! Per-thread persistent buffers grow lazily; ALL THREADPRIVATE arrays sized together.


250:             IF (.NOT. ALLOCATED(scratch_sym) .OR. .NOT. ALLOCATED(local_y) .OR. &


251:                 scratch_max_sym < n_symf_s) THEN


252:                IF (ALLOCATED(scratch_sym)) DEALLOCATE(scratch_sym)


253:                IF (ALLOCATED(scratch_forcetmp)) DEALLOCATE(scratch_forcetmp)


254:                IF (ALLOCATED(scratch_force3tmp)) DEALLOCATE(scratch_force3tmp)


255:                IF (ALLOCATED(local_y)) DEALLOCATE(local_y)


256:                IF (ALLOCATED(local_fi)) DEALLOCATE(local_fi)


257:                IF (ALLOCATED(local_stress)) DEALLOCATE(local_stress)


258:                ALLOCATE(scratch_sym(n_symf_s), scratch_forcetmp(3, n_symf_s), &


259:                         scratch_force3tmp(3, 3, n_symf_s))


260:                ALLOCATE(local_y(n_symf_s), local_fi(3, n_symf_s), local_stress(3, 3, n_symf_s))


261:                scratch_max_sym = n_symf_s


262:             END IF


263:             symtmp => scratch_sym(1:n_symf_s)


264:             forcetmp => scratch_forcetmp(:, 1:n_symf_s)


265:             ! Reset thread-local accumulators (only the used portion)


266:             local_y(1:n_symf_s) = 0.0_dp


267:             local_fi(:, 1:n_symf_s) = 0.0_dp


268:             IF (has_stress) local_stress(:, :, 1:n_symf_s) = 0.0_dp


269:             !$OMP DO SCHEDULE(static)


270:             DO j = 1, neighbor%n_rad(s)


271:                rvect1 = neighbor%dist_rad(1:3, j, s)


272:                r1 = neighbor%dist_rad(4, j, s)


273:                CALL nnp_calc_rad(nnp, ind, s, rvect1, r1, symtmp, forcetmp)


274:                jj = neighbor%ind_rad(j, s)


275:                DO sf = 1, n_symf_s


276:                   m = nnp%rad(ind)%symfgrp(s)%symf(sf)


277:                   DO l = 1, 3


278:                      local_fi(l, sf) = local_fi(l, sf) + forcetmp(l, sf)


279:                      !$OMP ATOMIC UPDATE


280:                      dsymdxyz(m, l, jj) = dsymdxyz(m, l, jj) - forcetmp(l, sf)


281:                   END DO


[...]


287:                      local_stress(2, 2, sf) = local_stress(2, 2, sf) + rvect1(2)*forcetmp(2, sf)


288:                      local_stress(3, 2, sf) = local_stress(3, 2, sf) + rvect1(3)*forcetmp(2, sf)


289:                      local_stress(1, 3, sf) = local_stress(1, 3, sf) + rvect1(1)*forcetmp(3, sf)


290:                      local_stress(2, 3, sf) = local_stress(2, 3, sf) + rvect1(2)*forcetmp(3, sf)


291:                      local_stress(3, 3, sf) = local_stress(3, 3, sf) + rvect1(3)*forcetmp(3, sf)


292:                   END IF


293:                   local_y(sf) = local_y(sf) + symtmp(sf)


294:                END DO


295:             END DO


296:             !$OMP END DO NOWAIT


297:             !$OMP CRITICAL


298:             DO sf = 1, n_symf_s


299:                m = nnp%rad(ind)%symfgrp(s)%symf(sf)


300:                DO l = 1, 3


301:                   dsymdxyz(m, l, i) = dsymdxyz(m, l, i) + local_fi(l, sf)


302:                END DO


303:                IF (has_stress) THEN


304:                   DO l = 1, 3


305:                      stress_p(1, l, m) = stress_p(1, l, m) + local_stress(1, l, sf)


306:                      stress_p(2, l, m) = stress_p(2, l, m) + local_stress(2, l, sf)


307:                      stress_p(3, l, m) = stress_p(3, l, m) + local_stress(3, l, sf)


308:                   END DO


309:                END IF


310:                nnp%rad(ind)%y(m) = nnp%rad(ind)%y(m) + local_y(sf)


311:             END DO


312:             !$OMP END CRITICAL


313:             !$OMP BARRIER


314:          END DO


315:          NULLIFY(symtmp)


316:          NULLIFY(forcetmp)


317:          !$OMP END PARALLEL


318:          CALL timestop(handle_sf)


319:          !loop over angular sym fnct grps


320:          CALL timeset('nnp_acsf_angular', handle_sf)


321:          off = nnp%n_rad(ind)


322:          !$OMP PARALLEL DEFAULT(NONE) &


323:          !$OMP&   SHARED(neighbor, nnp, ind, i, off, dsymdxyz, has_stress, stress_p, &


324:          !$OMP&          cut_type, spl_cos, spl_tanh, &


325:          !$OMP&          scratch_max_fc, scratch_fc1, scratch_dfc1, scratch_fc2, scratch_dfc2, &


326:          !$OMP&          scratch_dr1dx, scratch_dr2dx) &


327:          !$OMP&   PRIVATE(j, k, jj, kk, rvect1, rvect2, rvect3, r1, r2, r3, r3sq, r1inv, r2inv, &


328:          !$OMP&           sf, m, l, s, n_symf_s, fc_tmp, dfc_tmp, tanh_v, fc_needed, &


329:          !$OMP&           symtmp, force3tmp)


330:          DO s = 1, nnp%ang(ind)%n_symfgrp


331:             n_symf_s = nnp%ang(ind)%symfgrp(s)%n_symf


332:             ! Per-thread persistent buffers grow lazily; THREADPRIVATE arrays sized together.


333:             IF (.NOT. ALLOCATED(scratch_sym) .OR. .NOT. ALLOCATED(local_y) .OR. &


334:                 scratch_max_sym < n_symf_s) THEN


335:                IF (ALLOCATED(scratch_sym)) DEALLOCATE(scratch_sym)


336:                IF (ALLOCATED(scratch_forcetmp)) DEALLOCATE(scratch_forcetmp)


337:                IF (ALLOCATED(scratch_force3tmp)) DEALLOCATE(scratch_force3tmp)


338:                IF (ALLOCATED(local_y)) DEALLOCATE(local_y)


339:                IF (ALLOCATED(local_fi)) DEALLOCATE(local_fi)


340:                IF (ALLOCATED(local_stress)) DEALLOCATE(local_stress)


341:                ALLOCATE(scratch_sym(n_symf_s), scratch_forcetmp(3, n_symf_s), &


342:                         scratch_force3tmp(3, 3, n_symf_s))


343:                ALLOCATE(local_y(n_symf_s), local_fi(3, n_symf_s), local_stress(3, 3, n_symf_s))


344:                scratch_max_sym = n_symf_s


345:             END IF


346:             symtmp => scratch_sym(1:n_symf_s)


347:             force3tmp => scratch_force3tmp(:, :, 1:n_symf_s)


348:             ! Reset thread-local accumulators


349:             local_y(1:n_symf_s) = 0.0_dp


350:             local_fi(:, 1:n_symf_s) = 0.0_dp


351:             IF (has_stress) local_stress(:, :, 1:n_symf_s) = 0.0_dp


352: 


353:             ASSOCIATE (sg => nnp%ang(ind)%symfgrp(s))


354:             ! Hoist k-loop invariants (fc1/fc2/dr1dx/dr2dx) — these scratch arrays are SHARED


355:             ! across threads.  One thread does the realloc check under !$OMP SINGLE; the


356:             ! precompute itself is then split across the team via !$OMP DO so every thread


357:             ! does its share of the spline evaluations instead of waiting on a single thread.


358:             !$OMP SINGLE


359:             fc_needed = neighbor%n_ang1(s)


360:             IF (sg%ele(1) /= sg%ele(2)) fc_needed = MAX(fc_needed, neighbor%n_ang2(s))


361:             IF (scratch_max_fc < fc_needed) THEN


362:                IF (ALLOCATED(scratch_fc1)) &


363:                   DEALLOCATE(scratch_fc1, scratch_dfc1, scratch_fc2, scratch_dfc2, &


364:                              scratch_dr1dx, scratch_dr2dx)


365:                ALLOCATE(scratch_fc1(fc_needed), scratch_dfc1(fc_needed))


366:                ALLOCATE(scratch_fc2(fc_needed), scratch_dfc2(fc_needed))


367:                ALLOCATE(scratch_dr1dx(3, fc_needed), scratch_dr2dx(3, fc_needed))


368:                scratch_max_fc = fc_needed


369:             END IF


370:             !$OMP END SINGLE


371:             ! implicit barrier ensures the (re)allocation is visible to every thread


372:             SELECT CASE (cut_type)


373:             CASE (nnp_cut_cos)


374:                !$OMP DO SCHEDULE(static)


375:                DO j = 1, neighbor%n_ang1(s)


376:                   r1 = neighbor%dist_ang1(4, j, s)


377:                   fc_tmp = r1*sg%pi_over_cut


378:                   scratch_fc1(j) = 0.5_dp*(hermite_spline_value(spl_cos, fc_tmp, y1=dfc_tmp) + 1.0_dp)


379:                   scratch_dfc1(j) = 0.5_dp*dfc_tmp*sg%pi_over_cut


380:                   r1inv = 1.0_dp/r1


381:                   scratch_dr1dx(1, j) = neighbor%dist_ang1(1, j, s)*r1inv


382:                   scratch_dr1dx(2, j) = neighbor%dist_ang1(2, j, s)*r1inv


383:                   scratch_dr1dx(3, j) = neighbor%dist_ang1(3, j, s)*r1inv


384:                END DO


385:                !$OMP END DO


386:                IF (sg%ele(1) /= sg%ele(2)) THEN


387:                   !$OMP DO SCHEDULE(static)


388:                   DO k = 1, neighbor%n_ang2(s)


389:                      r2 = neighbor%dist_ang2(4, k, s)


390:                      fc_tmp = r2*sg%pi_over_cut


391:                      scratch_fc2(k) = 0.5_dp*(hermite_spline_value(spl_cos, fc_tmp, y1=dfc_tmp) + 1.0_dp)


392:                      scratch_dfc2(k) = 0.5_dp*dfc_tmp*sg%pi_over_cut


393:                      r2inv = 1.0_dp/r2


394:                      scratch_dr2dx(1, k) = neighbor%dist_ang2(1, k, s)*r2inv


395:                      scratch_dr2dx(2, k) = neighbor%dist_ang2(2, k, s)*r2inv


396:                      scratch_dr2dx(3, k) = neighbor%dist_ang2(3, k, s)*r2inv


397:                   END DO


398:                   !$OMP END DO


399:                END IF


400:             CASE (nnp_cut_tanh)


401:                !$OMP DO SCHEDULE(static)


402:                DO j = 1, neighbor%n_ang1(s)


403:                   r1 = neighbor%dist_ang1(4, j, s)


404:                   fc_tmp = 1.0_dp - r1*sg%inv_cut


405:                   tanh_v = hermite_spline_value(spl_tanh, fc_tmp)


406:                   scratch_fc1(j) = tanh_v**3


407:                   scratch_dfc1(j) = (-3.0_dp*sg%inv_cut)*(tanh_v**2 - tanh_v**4)


408:                   r1inv = 1.0_dp/r1


409:                   scratch_dr1dx(1, j) = neighbor%dist_ang1(1, j, s)*r1inv


410:                   scratch_dr1dx(2, j) = neighbor%dist_ang1(2, j, s)*r1inv


411:                   scratch_dr1dx(3, j) = neighbor%dist_ang1(3, j, s)*r1inv


412:                END DO


413:                !$OMP END DO


414:                IF (sg%ele(1) /= sg%ele(2)) THEN


415:                   !$OMP DO SCHEDULE(static)


416:                   DO k = 1, neighbor%n_ang2(s)


417:                      r2 = neighbor%dist_ang2(4, k, s)


418:                      fc_tmp = 1.0_dp - r2*sg%inv_cut


419:                      tanh_v = hermite_spline_value(spl_tanh, fc_tmp)


420:                      scratch_fc2(k) = tanh_v**3


421:                      scratch_dfc2(k) = (-3.0_dp*sg%inv_cut)*(tanh_v**2 - tanh_v**4)


422:                      r2inv = 1.0_dp/r2


423:                      scratch_dr2dx(1, k) = neighbor%dist_ang2(1, k, s)*r2inv


424:                      scratch_dr2dx(2, k) = neighbor%dist_ang2(2, k, s)*r2inv


425:                      scratch_dr2dx(3, k) = neighbor%dist_ang2(3, k, s)*r2inv


426:                   END DO


427:                   !$OMP END DO


428:                END IF


429:             END SELECT


430:             ! implicit barrier at end of last !$OMP DO publishes scratch_fc*/dr* to all threads


431: 


432:             IF (sg%ele(1) == sg%ele(2)) THEN


433:                ! Triangular k-loop (k = j+1..n_ang1) — can't COLLAPSE.  Earlier j has more


434:                ! work, so dynamic with a small chunk handles the imbalance with low overhead.


435:                !$OMP DO SCHEDULE(dynamic, 4)


436:                DO j = 1, neighbor%n_ang1(s)


437:                   rvect1 = neighbor%dist_ang1(1:3, j, s)


438:                   r1 = neighbor%dist_ang1(4, j, s)


439:                   DO k = j + 1, neighbor%n_ang1(s)


440:                      rvect2 = neighbor%dist_ang1(1:3, k, s)


441:                      r2 = neighbor%dist_ang1(4, k, s)


442:                      rvect3 = rvect2 - rvect1


443:                      r3sq = rvect3(1)*rvect3(1) + rvect3(2)*rvect3(2) + rvect3(3)*rvect3(3)


444:                      IF (r3sq < sg%cutoff_sq) THEN


445:                         r3 = SQRT(r3sq)


446:                         jj = neighbor%ind_ang1(j, s)


447:                         kk = neighbor%ind_ang1(k, s)


448:                         CALL nnp_calc_ang(nnp, ind, s, rvect1, rvect2, rvect3, &


449:                                           r1, r2, r3, symtmp, force3tmp, &


450:                                           fc1_in=scratch_fc1(j), fc2_in=scratch_fc1(k), &


451:                                           dfc1dr_in=scratch_dfc1(j), dfc2dr_in=scratch_dfc1(k), &


452:                                           dr1dx_in=scratch_dr1dx(:, j), dr2dx_in=scratch_dr1dx(:, k))


453:                         DO sf = 1, n_symf_s


454:                            m = off + sg%symf(sf)


455:                            DO l = 1, 3


456:                               local_fi(l, sf) = local_fi(l, sf) + force3tmp(l, 1, sf)


457:                               !$OMP ATOMIC UPDATE


458:                               dsymdxyz(m, l, jj) = dsymdxyz(m, l, jj) + force3tmp(l, 2, sf)


459:                               !$OMP ATOMIC UPDATE


460:                               dsymdxyz(m, l, kk) = dsymdxyz(m, l, kk) + force3tmp(l, 3, sf)


461:                            END DO


462:                            IF (has_stress) THEN


463:                               DO l = 1, 3


464:                                  local_stress(1, l, sf) = local_stress(1, l, sf) &


465:                                     - rvect1(1)*force3tmp(l, 2, sf) - rvect2(1)*force3tmp(l, 3, sf)


466:                                  local_stress(2, l, sf) = local_stress(2, l, sf) &


467:                                     - rvect1(2)*force3tmp(l, 2, sf) - rvect2(2)*force3tmp(l, 3, sf)


468:                                  local_stress(3, l, sf) = local_stress(3, l, sf) &


469:                                     - rvect1(3)*force3tmp(l, 2, sf) - rvect2(3)*force3tmp(l, 3, sf)


470:                               END DO


471:                            END IF


472:                            local_y(sf) = local_y(sf) + symtmp(sf)


473:                         END DO


474:                      END IF


475:                   END DO


476:                END DO


477:                !$OMP END DO NOWAIT


478:             ELSE


479:                ! Rectangular j-k loop — COLLAPSE(2) flattens the iteration space so threads


480:                ! stay busy when n_ang1 alone is too small for the team.  Loops must be


481:                ! perfectly nested to use COLLAPSE; the j-only rvect1/r1 reads are moved


482:                ! inside (cheap — they hit L1 cache).


483:                !$OMP DO COLLAPSE(2) SCHEDULE(dynamic, 16)


484:                DO j = 1, neighbor%n_ang1(s)


485:                   DO k = 1, neighbor%n_ang2(s)


486:                      rvect1 = neighbor%dist_ang1(1:3, j, s)


487:                      r1 = neighbor%dist_ang1(4, j, s)


488:                      rvect2 = neighbor%dist_ang2(1:3, k, s)


489:                      r2 = neighbor%dist_ang2(4, k, s)


490:                      rvect3 = rvect2 - rvect1


491:                      r3sq = rvect3(1)*rvect3(1) + rvect3(2)*rvect3(2) + rvect3(3)*rvect3(3)


492:                      IF (r3sq < sg%cutoff_sq) THEN


493:                         r3 = SQRT(r3sq)


494:                         jj = neighbor%ind_ang1(j, s)


495:                         kk = neighbor%ind_ang2(k, s)


496:                         CALL nnp_calc_ang(nnp, ind, s, rvect1, rvect2, rvect3, &


497:                                           r1, r2, r3, symtmp, force3tmp, &


498:                                           fc1_in=scratch_fc1(j), fc2_in=scratch_fc2(k), &


499:                                           dfc1dr_in=scratch_dfc1(j), dfc2dr_in=scratch_dfc2(k), &


500:                                           dr1dx_in=scratch_dr1dx(:, j), dr2dx_in=scratch_dr2dx(:, k))


501:                         DO sf = 1, n_symf_s


502:                            m = off + sg%symf(sf)


503:                            DO l = 1, 3


504:                               local_fi(l, sf) = local_fi(l, sf) + force3tmp(l, 1, sf)


505:                               !$OMP ATOMIC UPDATE


506:                               dsymdxyz(m, l, jj) = dsymdxyz(m, l, jj) + force3tmp(l, 2, sf)


507:                               !$OMP ATOMIC UPDATE


508:                               dsymdxyz(m, l, kk) = dsymdxyz(m, l, kk) + force3tmp(l, 3, sf)


509:                            END DO


510:                            IF (has_stress) THEN


511:                               DO l = 1, 3


512:                                  local_stress(1, l, sf) = local_stress(1, l, sf) &


513:                                     - rvect1(1)*force3tmp(l, 2, sf) - rvect2(1)*force3tmp(l, 3, sf)


514:                                  local_stress(2, l, sf) = local_stress(2, l, sf) &


515:                                     - rvect1(2)*force3tmp(l, 2, sf) - rvect2(2)*force3tmp(l, 3, sf)


516:                                  local_stress(3, l, sf) = local_stress(3, l, sf) &


517:                                     - rvect1(3)*force3tmp(l, 2, sf) - rvect2(3)*force3tmp(l, 3, sf)


518:                               END DO


519:                            END IF


520:                            local_y(sf) = local_y(sf) + symtmp(sf)


521:                         END DO


522:                      END IF


523:                   END DO


524:                END DO


525:                !$OMP END DO NOWAIT


526:             END IF


527:             !$OMP CRITICAL


528:             DO sf = 1, n_symf_s


529:                m = off + sg%symf(sf)


530:                DO l = 1, 3


531:                   dsymdxyz(m, l, i) = dsymdxyz(m, l, i) + local_fi(l, sf)


532:                END DO


533:                IF (has_stress) THEN


534:                   DO l = 1, 3


535:                      stress_p(1, l, m) = stress_p(1, l, m) + local_stress(1, l, sf)


536:                      stress_p(2, l, m) = stress_p(2, l, m) + local_stress(2, l, sf)


537:                      stress_p(3, l, m) = stress_p(3, l, m) + local_stress(3, l, sf)


538:                   END DO


539:                END IF


540:                nnp%ang(ind)%y(m - off) = nnp%ang(ind)%y(m - off) + local_y(sf)


541:             END DO


542:             !$OMP END CRITICAL


543:             !$OMP BARRIER


544:             END ASSOCIATE


545:          END DO


546:          NULLIFY(symtmp)


547:          NULLIFY(force3tmp)


548:          !$OMP END PARALLEL


549:          CALL timestop(handle_sf)


550:          !no forces


551:       ELSE


552:          !loop over radial sym fnct grps


553:          CALL timeset('nnp_acsf_radial', handle_sf)


554:          !$OMP PARALLEL DEFAULT(NONE) &


555:          !$OMP&   SHARED(neighbor, nnp, ind) &


556:          !$OMP&   PRIVATE(j, rvect1, r1, sf, m, s, n_symf_s, symtmp)


557:          DO s = 1, nnp%rad(ind)%n_symfgrp


558:             n_symf_s = nnp%rad(ind)%symfgrp(s)%n_symf


559:             IF (.NOT. ALLOCATED(scratch_sym) .OR. .NOT. ALLOCATED(local_y) .OR. &


560:                 scratch_max_sym < n_symf_s) THEN


561:                IF (ALLOCATED(scratch_sym)) DEALLOCATE(scratch_sym)


562:                IF (ALLOCATED(scratch_forcetmp)) DEALLOCATE(scratch_forcetmp)


563:                IF (ALLOCATED(scratch_force3tmp)) DEALLOCATE(scratch_force3tmp)


564:                IF (ALLOCATED(local_y)) DEALLOCATE(local_y)


565:                IF (ALLOCATED(local_fi)) DEALLOCATE(local_fi)


566:                IF (ALLOCATED(local_stress)) DEALLOCATE(local_stress)


567:                ALLOCATE(scratch_sym(n_symf_s), scratch_forcetmp(3, n_symf_s), &


568:                         scratch_force3tmp(3, 3, n_symf_s))


569:                ALLOCATE(local_y(n_symf_s), local_fi(3, n_symf_s), local_stress(3, 3, n_symf_s))


570:                scratch_max_sym = n_symf_s


571:             END IF


572:             symtmp => scratch_sym(1:n_symf_s)


573:             local_y(1:n_symf_s) = 0.0_dp


574:             !$OMP DO SCHEDULE(static)


575:             DO j = 1, neighbor%n_rad(s)


576:                rvect1 = neighbor%dist_rad(1:3, j, s)


577:                r1 = neighbor%dist_rad(4, j, s)


578:                CALL nnp_calc_rad(nnp, ind, s, rvect1, r1, symtmp)


579:                DO sf = 1, n_symf_s


580:                   local_y(sf) = local_y(sf) + symtmp(sf)


581:                END DO


582:             END DO


583:             !$OMP END DO NOWAIT


584:             !$OMP CRITICAL


585:             DO sf = 1, n_symf_s


586:                m = nnp%rad(ind)%symfgrp(s)%symf(sf)


587:                nnp%rad(ind)%y(m) = nnp%rad(ind)%y(m) + local_y(sf)


588:             END DO


589:             !$OMP END CRITICAL


590:             !$OMP BARRIER


591:          END DO

Code clean check


Detected a slowdown caused by scalar integer instructions (typically used for address computation).
By removing them, you can lower the cost of an iteration from 99.40 to 16.50 cycles (6.02x speedup).

Workaround


  • Try to reorganize arrays of structures to structures of arrays
  • Consider to permute loops (see vectorization gain report)
  • To reference allocatable arrays, use "allocatable" instead of "pointer" pointers or qualify them with the "contiguous" attribute (Fortran 2008)
  • For structures, limit to one indirection. For example, use a_b%c instead of a%b%c with a_b set to a%b before this function


Vectorization


Your function is probably not vectorized.
Only 12% of vector register length is used (average across all SSE/AVX instructions).


Details


Store and arithmetical SSE/AVX instructions are used in scalar version (process only one data element in vector registers).


Workaround


Read the "512-bits vectorization" report at "Potential" confidence level.

Execution units bottlenecks


Found no such bottlenecks but see expert reports for more complex bottlenecks.


  


512-bits vectorization


On some x86 processors supporting 512-bits vectorization, compilers are often too conservative and limit vectorization to 256 bits. Performance can then be improved by enforcing 512-bits vectorization, especially with many vectorized and high trip count loops. 512-bits vectorization performance overhead (compared to 256-bits) is generally lower on newer processors.


Workaround


Recompile with -qopt-zmm-usage=high


  


CALL instructions


Detected function call instructions.


Details


Calling (and then returning from) a function prevents many compiler optimizations (like vectorization), breaks control flow (which reduces pipeline performance) and executes extra instructions to save/restore the registers used inside it, which is very expensive (dozens of cycles). Consider to inline small functions.

Complex instructions


Detected COMPLEX INSTRUCTIONS.


Details


These instructions generate more than one micro-operation and only one of them can be decoded during a cycle and the extra micro-operations increase pressure on execution units.

Cycles and memory resources usage


Assuming all data fit into the L1 cache, each call to the function takes 99.40 cycles. At this rate:

  • 8% of peak load performance is reached (10.50 out of 128.00 bytes loaded per cycle (GB/s @ 1GHz))
  • 11% of peak store performance is reached (7.32 out of 64.00 bytes stored per cycle (GB/s @ 1GHz))


Front-end bottlenecks


Performance is limited by instruction throughput (loading/decoding program instructions to execution core) (front-end is a bottleneck).

By removing all these bottlenecks, you can lower the cost of an iteration from 99.40 to 73.00 cycles (1.36x speedup).

Code clean check


Detected a slowdown caused by scalar integer instructions (typically used for address computation).
By removing them, you can lower the cost of an iteration from 99.40 to 16.50 cycles (6.02x speedup).

Workaround


  • Try to reorganize arrays of structures to structures of arrays
  • Consider to permute loops (see vectorization gain report)
  • To reference allocatable arrays, use "allocatable" instead of "pointer" pointers or qualify them with the "contiguous" attribute (Fortran 2008)
  • For structures, limit to one indirection. For example, use a_b%c instead of a%b%c with a_b set to a%b before this function


Vectorization


Your function is probably not vectorized.
Only 12% of vector register length is used (average across all SSE/AVX instructions).


Details


Store and arithmetical SSE/AVX instructions are used in scalar version (process only one data element in vector registers).


Workaround


Read the "512-bits vectorization" report at "Potential" confidence level.

Execution units bottlenecks


Found no such bottlenecks but see expert reports for more complex bottlenecks.


  


512-bits vectorization


On some x86 processors supporting 512-bits vectorization, compilers are often too conservative and limit vectorization to 256 bits. Performance can then be improved by enforcing 512-bits vectorization, especially with many vectorized and high trip count loops. 512-bits vectorization performance overhead (compared to 256-bits) is generally lower on newer processors.


Workaround


Recompile with -qopt-zmm-usage=high


  


CALL instructions


Detected function call instructions.


Details


Calling (and then returning from) a function prevents many compiler optimizations (like vectorization), breaks control flow (which reduces pipeline performance) and executes extra instructions to save/restore the registers used inside it, which is very expensive (dozens of cycles). Consider to inline small functions.

Complex instructions


Detected COMPLEX INSTRUCTIONS.


Details


These instructions generate more than one micro-operation and only one of them can be decoded during a cycle and the extra micro-operations increase pressure on execution units.

Cycles and memory resources usage


Assuming all data fit into the L1 cache, each call to the function takes 99.40 cycles. At this rate:

  • 8% of peak load performance is reached (10.50 out of 128.00 bytes loaded per cycle (GB/s @ 1GHz))
  • 11% of peak store performance is reached (7.32 out of 64.00 bytes stored per cycle (GB/s @ 1GHz))


Front-end bottlenecks


Performance is limited by instruction throughput (loading/decoding program instructions to execution core) (front-end is a bottleneck).

By removing all these bottlenecks, you can lower the cost of an iteration from 99.40 to 73.00 cycles (1.36x speedup).
